questions.question: Explain the memory representation of arrays with a suitable example. How is the address of any element calculated?
questions.modelAnswer: Arrays store elements in contiguous memory. Address of arr[i] = Base_Address + (i × Element_Size). Example: For int arr[5] at base 1000, arr[3] = 1000 + 3×4 = 1012.

questions.question: Compare the time complexity of various array operations. Why is insertion at the beginning O(n)?
keyPoints: Search: O(n) unsorted, O(log n) sorted
Insert at beginning: O(n) - shift all elements

questions.question: What are the advantages and disadvantages of arrays? When should you use arrays over other data structures?
keyPoints: Advantages: O(1) access, cache friendly, memory efficient
Use when: size known, frequent access needed
Avoid when: dynamic size, frequent insertions

questions.question: Write a program to find the largest and smallest elements in an array.
keyPoints: Initialize max and min with first element

questions.question: Differentiate between static arrays and dynamic arrays. Give examples.
keyPoints: Static: fixed size at compile time
Examples: C arrays (static), ArrayList in Java (dynamic)
